description:
The quiz assesses proficiency in core data structures such as stacks, lists, and trees, as well as algorithmic concepts and time‑complexity analysis. It is moderately challenging, suitable for learners who have completed introductory coursework and are ready to deepen their understanding. The tone is instructional, guiding participants through a series of 20 focused questions.
Topics: Stacks, Lists, Trees, Algorithm design, Time complexity, Big O notation
Audience: Students and early‑career developers who have completed basic programming courses and want to solidify their data structures and algorithms knowledge.
